acne.description: Acne is a common skin condition where hair follicles become clogged with oil and dead skin cells, often appearing as pimples, blackheads, or whiteheads.
care_tips: Wash affected areas gently twice daily with a mild cleanser
Avoid picking or squeezing lesions to reduce scarring risk
Use non-comedogenic moisturizers and sunscreen
Keep hair and phone surfaces clean if they touch your face
acne.when_to_see_doctor: See a dermatologist if acne is painful, leaves scars, does not improve after several weeks of basic care, or affects your quality of life.

benign.description: Benign skin growths are non-cancerous lesions such as moles, cysts, or other harmless tissue changes. Many people have them and they are often stable over time.
care_tips: Monitor for changes using the ABCDE rule (Asymmetry, Border, Color, Diameter, Evolution)
Take dated photos if you want to track changes over time
Use sun protection on exposed skin
Do not attempt to remove or treat growths at home
benign.when_to_see_doctor: Consult a dermatologist if a growth changes shape, color, size, bleeds, itches persistently, or looks different from your other spots.

eczema.description: Eczema (atopic dermatitis) is an inflammatory skin condition that causes dry, itchy, and sometimes red or cracked patches of skin.
care_tips: Moisturize regularly, especially after bathing
Use fragrance-free products and lukewarm (not hot) showers
Identify and avoid personal triggers such as harsh soaps or certain fabrics
Keep nails trimmed to reduce damage from scratching
eczema.when_to_see_doctor: Seek medical advice if itching disrupts sleep, skin becomes infected (oozing, crusting, fever), or over-the-counter care is not helping.

fungal.description: Fungal skin infections are caused by fungi growing on the skin surface. Common examples include ringworm and athlete's foot, often appearing as scaly or ring-shaped rashes.
care_tips: Keep affected areas clean and dry
Avoid sharing towels, clothing, or footwear
Change socks and underwear daily
Do not use steroid creams on suspected fungal rashes without medical guidance
fungal.when_to_see_doctor: See a doctor if the rash spreads, does not improve within 2 weeks, affects nails or scalp, or you have diabetes or a weakened immune system.

melanoma.description: Melanoma is a serious form of skin cancer that develops in pigment-producing cells. Early detection is critical — any suspicious mole or lesion should be evaluated promptly by a professional.
care_tips: Do not delay professional evaluation for suspicious lesions
Avoid sun exposure on the affected area until assessed
Document the lesion with a dated photo for your doctor
This tool cannot diagnose cancer — only a dermatologist can
melanoma.when_to_see_doctor: Seek urgent dermatology evaluation as soon as possible. Melanoma requires professional diagnosis and treatment — do not wait.
